symptoms.symptom_name: Instantaneous exertional cramping knot relieved by stretch

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Instantaneous exertional onset with stretch relief is characteristic of cramp.
DVT is gradual, not an instantaneous stretch-relieved knot.

symptoms.symptom_name: No diffuse swelling/warmth, no DVT risk factors

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of diffuse swelling/warmth and VTE risk factors gives a low Wells score.

symptoms.symptom_name: Warm perfused foot, normal sensation/movement, no tense compartment

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal perfusion/neuro and no pain-out-of-proportion argue strongly against compartment syndrome/ischaemia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Heavy sweating / relative dehydration during hot endurance exercise

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fatigue and fluid/electrolyte depletion contribute to exertional cramp.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bounding equal distal pulses, cap refill <2s (examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Intact pulses/perfusion argue against ischaemia/compartment syndrome but require examination.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Benign cramp needs none; conservative care.
Only if DVT features develop (diffuse swelling, warmth, rest tenderness).
Only if dark urine / suspected rhabdomyolysis.
